current_conditions.site_name: ETOWAH RIVER AT GA 9, NEAR DAWSONVILLE, GA

water_temp_read.read: Water is very warm for trout.

light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

caution_notes: Warm water may increase trout stress.

generated_text.quick_read: Trout fishing on the Etowah River is tough due to very warm water temperatures near 82°F. Recent stocking supports some bite, but behavior will be cautious.
generated_text.best_window: Morning of day 1 is best because cooler temperatures and moderate humidity create the most comfortable conditions for trout, improving feeding activity despite overall warm water.
generated_text.current_conditions: Water temperature is 82°F, flow is 108 cfs, gage height is 3.77 feet, and turbidity is 8.7 NTU as of the latest USGS reading.
generated_text.weather_setup: Sky cover is partially cloudy around 22%, pressure steady at 30.09 inHg with no clear trend, light wind averaging 1.6 mph, and low rain probability with no expected rainfall.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will seek cold-water refuges and oxygenated pockets, positioning near runs, seams, and structure. Use small natural patterns and stocked trout attractors for clean drifts. Present nymphs delicately as trout are stressed by the warm water; caution is advised due to increased stress on fish.
